analysis_result: 35 samples in the MOESM4 Clinical Data table were classified with Tumor Type == 'Neurofibroma' (non-cutaneous neurofibromas). This differs from the paper's reported 34 plexiform neurofibromas by 1 sample. The closest possible matches: (a) Neurofibroma Tumor Type = 35 samples (19 Peripheral + 16 Spinal), (b) Neurofibroma with Location=Peripheral only = 19 samples, (c) Neurofibroma + Atypical_NF non-cutaneous = 56 samples. No combination yields exactly 34 in the supplied table; the 35 non-cutaneous Neurofibroma count is the nearest match and most likely corresponds to the paper's 34 (possibly differing due to one sample reclassification or exclusion not documented in the supplementary table).
denominator_used: N = 108 samples in MOESM4 Clinical Data (filtered to non-null Tumor_ID); subset examined: Tumor Type == 'Neurofibroma' yielding N = 35.
assumptions_made: 'Plexiform neurofibroma' maps to Tumor Type == 'Neurofibroma' (since Cutaneous_NF is a separate Tumor Type value and all 33 Cutaneous_NF samples have Location='Cutaneous'); filtered to rows with non-null Tumor_ID per data context instructions; did not consider Atypical_NF (n=21) or Low-Grade MPNST (n=3) as plexiform neurofibromas.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: MOESM4 Clinical Data sheet, 108 rows x 15 columns after filtering to non-null Tumor_ID. Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Location, Methylation_Group.

B. COHORT DEFINITION: All 108 samples in Clinical Data with non-null Tumor_ID.

C. VARIABLE CODING: Tumor Type distribution: Neurofibroma (35), Cutaneous_NF (33), Atypical_NF (21), MPNST (16), Low-Grade MPNST (3). Location distribution: Peripheral (46), Cutaneous (33), Spinal (29). Cross-tab shows Cutaneous_NF is 100% Cutaneous location; Neurofibroma split into 19 Peripheral + 16 Spinal. 'Plexiform neurofibroma' interpreted as the non-cutaneous Neurofibroma Tumor Type category (excludes Cutaneous_NF, Atypical_NF, MPNST, Low-Grade MPNST).

D. COMPUTATION: Count of rows where Tumor Type == 'Neurofibroma' = 35. Breakdown by Methylation_Group: Benign_NF-G4 = 20, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 = 9, PremalignantNF_G3 = 6. Breakdown by NF1 Status: NF1 and Sporadic mixed across all 35.

E. RESULT: 35 samples classified as Neurofibroma (Tumor Type) in MOESM4 Clinical Data. Paper states 34, so there is a 1-sample discrepancy; the supplementary table does not match the paper text exactly. Other interpretations: 19 Peripheral-only Neurofibromas; 35 Neurofibromas + 21 Atypical_NF = 56 non-cutaneous peripheral nerve sheath tumors. None of these equal 34 exactly.

F. CAVEATS: Paper's exact definition of 'plexiform' may differ from the Tumor Type label. The 6 PremalignantNF_G3 samples within Neurofibroma Tumor Type may include ANNUBP/atypical hybrids. Could not resolve the 34 vs 35 discrepancy from MOESM4 alone; the paper may exclude one specific sample (e.g., a spinal sample re-classified) that is not flagged in the supplementary table.
